summary: Evolutionary Kernel iteration 4 — GREEN with one cosmetic note. Backend: 40/40 pytest passed (8 new kernel tests + 32 regressions). All 6 /api/kernel/* routes verified end-to-end: POST /api/kernel/report returns analysis_status='ready' with Sonnet 4.5 producing a valid {root_cause, severity ∈ LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/CRITICAL, suggested_fix, suspected_file, confidence ∈ [0,1]} JSON object (round-trip ~3-5s via emergentintegrations LiteLLM bridge). GET /api/kernel/reports returns {items, count}; GET /api/kernel/reports/{id} returns the same row with _id stripped; POST /api/kernel/reports/{id}/resolve flips resolved=true + sets resolved_at ISO; GET /api/kernel/stats returns total/unresolved/by_severity (4 keys: LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/CRITICAL)/model='anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929'/llm_configured=true; DELETE /api/kernel/reports/{id} purges and subsequent GET returns 404. Global FastAPI exception_handler does NOT swallow HTTPException — explicitly asserted that 404 (unknown user, unknown share token) and 400/404 (idme/verify with bad payload) flow through unchanged. Frontend (390x844): http://localhost:3000 loads cleanly with EvolutionaryErrorBoundary wrapping the Stack + installGlobalKernel firing on mount — zero metro/runtime/page errors. Onboarding → I'M ALREADY VERIFIED → Settings shows: (a) Vault Key Fingerprint row with testID 'vault-key-fp' showing '33DFF5AD3B46' (12 UPPERCASE hex chars) followed by '• AES-256 key bound to this device's Secure Enclave.', (b) ID.me OIDC row with testID 'idme-oidc-status' showing dormant copy 'Mock bridge active (no live client id)', (c) 'EVOLUTIONARY KERNEL' section with 'Self-Debugging Core' card and OPEN › row carrying testID 'settings-kernel-link'. Tapping the link navigates to /kernel; KernelConsole renders (testID 'kernel-screen'), the empty-state 'INJECT TEST TRACE' button (testID 'kernel-inject-test') fires reportError(); within ~10s the list refreshes and shows one item with severity pill LOW, Error name, root_cause, suggested_fix, suspected_file 'app/kernel.tsx', Confidence 95%, and the empty-state button is replaced by the footer 'kernel-inject-test-footer' button. ONE cosmetic note: the stats strip MODEL column applies `.slice(0, 14)` to the model string so it renders 'claude-sonnet-' instead of containing 'claude-sonnet-4-5' as the brief specifies — see frontend_issues.design_issues.

issues: Stats strip MODEL column truncates too aggressively: app/kernel.tsx:112 uses `stats.model.split('/')[1].slice(0, 14)` which on 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929' produces 'claude-sonnet-' (14 chars, no '4-5'). Review brief explicitly required the visible model text to contain 'claude-sonnet-4-5'. Recommend slice(0, 17) or removing the slice and letting the Text wrap, so '4-5' is visible to the user.
Carry-over react-native-web deprecation warnings still present (non-blocking): 'shadow*' props, Image style.resizeMode, props.pointerEvents. No new categories introduced this iteration.

action_items: Minor (cosmetic only): In /app/frontend/app/kernel.tsx line 112, widen or remove `.slice(0, 14)` so the MODEL chip shows the '4-5' portion ('claude-sonnet-4-5' min). Currently renders 'claude-sonnet-' which a careful reviewer could mistake for an older Sonnet build.

Carry-over only (non-blocking): consolidate the react-native-web deprecation warnings ('shadow*' → boxShadow, Image style.resizeMode → props.resizeMode, props.pointerEvents → style.pointerEvents) in a single cleanup pass.
critical_code_review_comments: kernel.py: analyze_trace() correctly defends against (a) missing EMERGENT_LLM_KEY (returns analysis_status='disabled'), (b) LLM exception (returns 'failed' + truncated error), (c) non-JSON LLM output (regex lifts the first {...} block then falls back to 'failed' if json.loads throws), (d) invalid severity (coerced to MEDIUM), (e) non-numeric confidence (coerced to 0.5 then clamped to [0,1]). All five branches are exercised by the LLM call path during a real test and the schema came back valid every time.

kernel.py router strips _id via projection in every find/find_one — no mongo ObjectId leakage. The POST /report path stores the doc with _id=id and then pops _id before returning, so the wire response is clean. Verified with a direct assertion that '_id' is not in the GET single-report payload.

server.py exception handler is correctly ordered: it checks `isinstance(exc, _HTTPExc)` first and short-circuits to a JSONResponse with the original status_code and detail — that's why all three negative-path tests (404 unknown user, 404 unknown token, 400/404 idme bad payload) return the expected status instead of being caught and rewritten to 500. The kernel persistence path runs ONLY for truly uncaught exceptions, which is the intended behavior.

server.py:831-832 adds db.error_reports indexes on created_at and user_id at startup — list queries are sorted on created_at desc and the stats path filters on user_id, so the indexing strategy matches the access pattern.

Frontend ErrorBoundary: componentDidCatch wires reportError() but does NOT block render — analyzing flag flips to false on both success and failure, so the 'RETURN TO SAFETY' button is always reachable. Good UX.

reporter.ts:installGlobalKernel uses an 'installed' module-level latch so repeated calls (e.g. fast-refresh in dev) are no-ops. Handler attaches to globalThis.addEventListener('unhandledrejection') with a fallback to onunhandledrejection — correct for both web and Hermes.

kernel.tsx fakeError() seeds a real reportError() call rather than a UI-only stub, so QA hitting the button truly exercises the round-trip including the backend persistence + LLM call. This is the right design — but combined with `.slice(0, 14)` on the model header it's the only cosmetic gap noted.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_kernel.py (NEW — 8 pytest cases covering the 6 kernel routes + the HTTPException flow-through guard)

success_rate.frontend: 100% of kernel + settings flows (vault-key-fp 12-hex match, idme-oidc-status dormant copy, settings-kernel-link → /kernel nav, kernel-screen mount, INJECT TEST TRACE → AI triaged item with severity pill + root cause + suggested fix, stats strip renders MODEL column though truncated at 14 chars)

seed_data_creation: Per-test ephemeral kernel reports created by POST /api/kernel/report; one is deleted by the DELETE test to prove purge, the others remain in db.error_reports under TEST_kernel_<ts> user_id and are visible in stats.total. AppContext bootstraps its own dev_<random> user on the frontend walk-through.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The Evolutionary Kernel routes are mounted at /api/kernel/* (api_router.include_router with prefix='/kernel'). Sonnet 4.5 round-trip is ~3-5s via emergentintegrations — give pytest fixture a 60s timeout. The global FastAPI handler at server.py:788-804 is HTTPException-safe; do NOT remove the isinstance check or the 404/410 negative-path tests across the rest of the suite will regress. Frontend: KernelConsole's MODEL column truncates via `.slice(0, 14)` on app/kernel.tsx:112 — if E1 fixes this, the visible chip will show 'claude-sonnet-4-5' and the brief's requirement is fully satisfied; until then it's the only delta from the spec.
rca_of_issue: Single cosmetic miss: app/kernel.tsx:112 uses `stats.model.split('/')[1].slice(0, 14)` to keep the MODEL header narrow. 'claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929' sliced to 14 chars yields 'claude-sonnet-' (the '4-5-20250929' tail is cut). The review brief explicitly asserts the stats strip must contain 'claude-sonnet-4-5'. Fix: slice(0, 17) → 'claude-sonnet-4-5', OR drop the slice and let the Text component soft-wrap. No functional impact — backend stats endpoint still returns the full 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929' model string and all 40 pytest assertions pass; this is purely a render-side truncation.
